hypothesis: If tensor completion methods (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensemble) are used as surrogate models for partially observed lattice-design tensors, then they will achieve higher R² predictive accuracy for Young's modulus and mass-normalized Young's modulus than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines—particularly under limited or non-uniform (biased) training sampling—because tensor completion exploits multi-way correlations among design parameters.
